fact: October 25 is exactly 67 days before the end of the year - that's almost 10 whole weeks of time left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now and can hide up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun is setting so much earlier that kids in northern places like Alaska only get about 7 hours of daylight - it's like living in a twilight world!
October 25 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when the mysterious scorpion rules the zodiac with its stinger ready to strike!
Maple trees are dumping their leaves so fast right now that a single tree can drop 200,000 leaves in just a few weeks!
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere indoors? Because October 25 is prime time for house spiders to sneak inside looking for warm winter hideouts!
The moon around this date can look absolutely massive and orange when it rises - that's because autumn air is full of tiny particles that make it glow like a giant pumpkin!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 25 means spring is exploding with baby animals being born everywhere - it's like nature's nursery!
Morning frost starts appearing on grass around this time in cold places, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that look like nature's glitter!
Geese flying south right now can travel 1,500 miles without stopping - they're like feathered jet planes honking across the sky!
October 25 sits exactly 33 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're officially deep into the season when everything goes bonkers with color!
Bears in northern forests are eating up to 20,000 calories per day right now - that's like gobbling down 40 hamburgers every single day to get ready for winter!
